detailedExemplarData.title: Pauta de rehabilitaciÃ³n, como el alcohol afecta al desarrollo neurolÃ³gico de los adolescentes.

strengths: Robust information-literacy demonstrated by compiling detailed data on rehabilitation centers with full APA citations
Effective communication and collaboration skills shown through interviews with experts (neurologist, psychologist) and empathy in stakeholder engagement
Strong self-management practices illustrated by proactive weekly planning and task division
Thoughtful design choices and visual planning to enhance audience engagement
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The student shows a high level of ATL skill application: research is thorough and properly cited, interviews enrich content and demonstrate empathy, time-management is disciplined, and communication strategies are clearly tailored to the audience. There are no major gaps in the description of how skills were used to achieve the product.

strengths: Clear articulation of a personal connection and motivation, demonstrating why the project matters to the studentâs context
Well-defined learning goal linked to real-world relevance of adolescent alcohol impacts
weaknesses: Action timeline lacks detail about required resources and clear sequencing rationale
Absence of measurable checkpoints or interim milestones to track progress
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The planning section excels in establishing a convincing personal rationale and a focused learning goal, grounded in the studentâs environment. However, the feasibility of the plan is undermined by a high-level timeline that omits critical details such as resource needs, explicit task dependencies and measurable benchmarks to monitor progress.

strengths: In-depth analysis of social and emotional drivers of underage drinking, supported by interview evidence
Insightful personal reflections on increased empathy, mental health awareness and overcoming procrastination
Thoughtful ethical considerations, adapting methods to respect privacy constraints
Strong evaluation of product pivot, showing responsiveness to audience needs
weaknesses: Reflection addresses only some success criteria, leaving others unexamined
Lacks discussion of how unmet scientific-journal criterion and statistics criterion affect the final product or plans for remediation
Incomplete justifications for strengths and weaknesses in product evaluation and specialist interview challenges
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The reflection is rich and introspective, offering clear examples of personal growth, ethical awareness and a strategic shift in product design. However, it falls short of a comprehensive evaluation: several success criteria are not revisited, the impact of unmet standards is unaddressed, and proposed next steps are not fully articulated.

annotations.comment: Compiling detailed information on two Chilean rehabilitation centers and citing their contacts shows effective information-literacy and ATL research skills (Applying skills).
Your action timeline outlines weekly tasks, but lacks details on required resources, explicit sequencing rationale and measurable checkpoints to fully support feasibility (Planning).
Your reflection on conducting interviews showcases developed communication and empathy ATL skills, using participant insights to deepen your productâs relevance (Applying skills).
Your analysis of social and emotional drivers of underage drinking is detailed and grounded in interview evidence, enhancing the depth of your reflection (Reflecting).

You articulate a clear personal connection to your project by explaining its relevance in your environment and motivation, strengthening your rationale and evidence for exploring adolescent alcohol impacts (Planning).
You describe precise self-management strategiesâsuch as weekly planning and proactive task divisionâthat demonstrate effective ATL time-management skills (Applying skills).
You note the scientific-journal criterion as unmet yet your reflection doesnât discuss how this gap affected your product or plan for future research (Reflecting).
Your systematic collection and APA citation of multiple sources in your initial evidence doc demonstrates strong information-literacy ATL skills (Applying skills).
You provide vivid examples of how your research fostered empathy and analytical observation of adolescent alcohol use, supporting your reflection with firsthand evidence (Reflecting).
Your description of becoming more conscious about mental health demonstrates meaningful personal impact, backed by specific examples of changed awareness (Reflecting).
You clearly articulate how you overcame procrastination through disciplined scheduling and goal-setting, providing concrete evidence of behavioral change (Reflecting).
Your summary insightfully connects empathy, responsibility and proactive habits, showing holistic growth as both student and individual (Reflecting).
You thoroughly reflect on how your perspective shifted regarding adolescent alcohol myths, linking new understanding to credible research (Reflecting).
Your product evaluation addresses only the first success criterion; other criteria lack review or evidence, limiting the completeness of your reflection (Reflecting).
You describe strengths and weaknesses for the alcohol-effects criterion, but the justification is unfinished and missing specific examples or outcomes (Reflecting).
Your evaluation of specialist interview criteria notes challenges but lacks reflection on how those issues influenced your final product quality (Reflecting).
You recognize the statistics criterion wasnât met, but you havenât explored alternatives or proposed remedial steps in your reflection (Reflecting).
Your discussion of design choicesâusing a friendly, engaging format for parents and teensâdemonstrates applying communication ATL skills to maximize audience impact (Applying skills).
Your visual presentation planningâstructure of slides and consistent designâshows effective application of communication ATL skills to enhance outreach (Applying skills).
Your interviews with a neurologist and psychologist, including technical and behavioral insights, reflect strong collaboration and ATL research skills (Applying skills).
You reflect thoughtfully on ethical constraintsâsuch as patient privacyâand adapted your evidence-gathering methods accordingly, illustrating mature reflective practice (Reflecting).
Your rationale for pivoting from a detailed report to a practical rehabilitation guide shows strong evaluation of audience needs and product purpose (Reflecting).
